SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
Account
The account.
AccountConsumer
The Kafka consumer for the payload with account in JSON format.
AccountM
The account in MongoDB.
AccountMongoService
The service for the MongoDB database.

This service uses the 'Active Record' design pattern.
The Quarkus guide proposes two solutions for MongoDB: using the 'Active Record' pattern using the 'Repository' pattern
AccountP
The account in PostgreSQL.
AccountPostgresService
The service for the PostgreSQL database.
AccountProducer
The Account producer.
AccountRepository
The PanacheRepository implementation for the AccountP.
AccountResource
Quarkus uses JAX-RS to define REST-ful web APIs.

It is now recommended to use RESTEasy Reactive, which supports both traditional blocking workloads and reactive workloads equally well.
Writing JSON REST Services
Writing REST Services with RESTEasy Reactive
AccountSerializer
The Serializer implementation for the Account
Application
The main class for the account sender.
Constants
The constants.
Constants
The constants.
Money
The money in account.
MoneyM
The money in MongoDB.
MoneyP
The money in PostgreSQL.
MoneyRepository
The PanacheRepository implementation for the MoneyP.
Owner
The owner of the account.
OwnerM
The account owner in MongoDB.
OwnerP
The account owner in PostgreSQL.
OwnerRepository
The PanacheRepository implementation for the OwnerP.
RecordKey
The record key from Kafka message.
Status
The status
Status
The account status
Utils
The utilities.
